title: chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. axillaris
intro: Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. axillaris, also known as the Bigelow's sneezeweed or thread-leaf sneezeweed, is a subspecies of the shrubby Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to its high concentration of volatile oils. The health benefits of Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. axillaris include relief from colds, fever, and respiratory issues, as well as its use in treating skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making infusions or decoctions from the leaves and flowers, which can be used internally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
